obsolete_functionality.reason: Duplicate entries: venv/ appears on lines 9 and 41, env/ appears on lines 8 and 42, *.log appears on lines 33 and 89. These pre-existing duplicates inflate the file and create maintenance confusion.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Remove the second occurrence of each duplicate entry. The duplicates predate this diff (only line 104 was changed from workflow-current.json to workflow-*.json).

obsolete_functionality.reason: Duplicate entries: venv/ appears on lines 12 and 44, env/ appears on lines 11 and 45, *.log appears at lines 36 and 92. Pre-existing duplicates identical to knowledge-system/.gitignore pattern.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Remove the second occurrence of each duplicate entry. Only workflow-current.json -> workflow-*.json was changed in this diff.

obsolete_functionality.reason: Duplicate entry: venv/ appears on lines 1, 2 (**/venv/ covers it), and 31. Line 1 (venv/) is made redundant by line 2 (**/venv/), and line 31 repeats line 1. Pre-existing issue.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Keep only **/venv/ (line 2) which covers both root-level and nested venv directories. Remove plain venv/ duplicates at lines 1 and 31.

obsolete_functionality.reason: Redundant pattern: both .codex (file pattern, line 12) and .codex/ (directory pattern, line 13) are listed. The new diff added the bare .codex entry. Since .codex is a directory (created by the Codex CLI), .codex/ alone is sufficient. The bare .codex also matches any file named .codex which is not the intended target.
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Remove bare .codex (line 12) and keep .codex/ only. If the Codex CLI ever creates a plain .codex file alongside the directory, add the file pattern back with a comment explaining both forms.

obsolete_functionality.reason: Four directory names (education_details, experience_details, projects, skills) are listed without trailing slashes. These are all directories on disk, not files. Without trailing slashes, the patterns also match files with those exact names, creating ambiguity. Additionally *.backup appears twice (lines exist in both the pre-diff and new content).
obsolete_functionality.recommendation: Add trailing slashes: education_details/, experience_details/, projects/, skills/. This makes the intent explicit and avoids accidentally ignoring hypothetical files with those names. Also de-duplicate the *.backup entry.

duplicate_content.reason: knowledge-system/.gitignore and knowledge-system-qijie/.gitignore are near-identical in structure and content (same duplicate venv/env/log pattern, same public-repo exclusions section, same Playwright MCP and workflow sections). The only material difference is knowledge-system-qijie adds .env at the top.
duplicate_content.recommendation: Advisory only: consider whether the two repos can share a common .gitignore base. Not a blocking issue.

dot_claude_gitignore.changes: Added: .last-cleanup, playwright-storage-state.json, .todos.json, docs/, and settings.json.bak-* to ignored patterns.
dot_claude_gitignore.assessment: Clean and appropriate. docs/ is correct to ignore since it holds transient dev-cycle output that is not version-controlled (git ls-files docs/ returns 0 tracked files). The new entries are all runtime artifacts.

knowledge_system_gitignore.assessment: Correct improvement. Pre-existing duplicate entries (venv/, env/, *.log) are advisory-only findings not introduced by this diff.

knowledge_system_qijie_gitignore.assessment: Correct improvement. Same pre-existing duplicate pattern as knowledge-system.

multi_asset_portfolio_gitignore.changes: Added .codex, .playwright-mcp/, qa-*.png, and broadened workflow pattern to wildcard.
multi_asset_portfolio_gitignore.assessment: Additions are appropriate runtime artifact exclusions. Pre-existing venv/ duplication is advisory.

orchestra_gitignore.changes: Added bare .codex entry alongside existing .codex/ entry.
orchestra_gitignore.assessment: Minor issue introduced in this diff: bare .codex and .codex/ are redundant. .codex/ alone covers the Codex CLI directory artifact.

budget_management_gitignore.assessment: Clean additions. All are appropriate runtime and build artifact exclusions.

travel_planner_gitignore.changes: Broadened data/*/*.bak to *.bak (global), added *.png, *.jpg, .playwright-mcp/, and broadened workflow pattern.
travel_planner_gitignore.assessment: Broadening *.bak to global scope is intentional (removes path restriction). Other additions are clean.

happy_gitignore.assessment: Clean additions. Lock files are legitimately machine-specific in this monorepo context; the comment clarifies intent.

rednote_mcp_gitignore.assessment: INDEX.md is a generated auto-updated file (confirmed: not tracked in git, has auto-timestamp header). Ignoring it is correct. .claude/ suppresses the entire Claude config directory which is appropriate for this repo's scope. Additions are clean.

excel_analyzer_gitignore.assessment: Clean addition. Note: workflow pattern remains .claude/workflow-current.json (not broadened to wildcard like other repos). This is a scope gap but not a cleanliness violation of this diff.

application_assistant_gitignore.changes: Added null/, education_details, experience_details, projects, skills.
application_assistant_gitignore.assessment: The null/ entry is correctly documented as a bug artifact directory. The four data directory patterns lack trailing slashes (minor issue introduced in this diff). *.backup duplication is pre-existing.
issues: directory patterns without trailing slashes (minor, introduced in this diff)

summary.notes: All changed files are .gitignore configuration files. No misplaced documents, temp files, build artifacts, orphaned scripts, or duplicate binaries were found in the diff scope. The two issues introduced in this diff are minor style concerns (redundant .codex pattern in orchestra, missing trailing slashes in application-assistant). The three pre-existing issues (duplicate venv/env/log entries in knowledge-system and knowledge-system-qijie, and duplicate venv/ in multi-asset-portfolio) are advisory and non-blocking per the default-safe rule for pre-existing violations.
